faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth problem in my Harlem apartment?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, webbing in closet corners, tiny moths flying around wardrobes, and larvae or cocoons on clothing or carpets. You may also notice damage to upholstered furniture, rugs, or other natural fiber materials in your home.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth treatment cost in Harlem?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Harlem typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the number of treatments required. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes so you know exactly what to expect before we begin treatment.

faqs.question: How long does it take to eliminate a clothes moth infestation?
faqs.answer: Most clothes moth infestations require 2-3 treatments spaced several weeks apart to completely eliminate all life stages, from eggs to adults. The total process typically takes 6-8 weeks, with many clients noticing significant improvement after the first treatment. We also provide prevention tips to keep moths from returning.

faqs.question: Are clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets in my Harlem home?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and application methods that are safe for your family and pets when applied by our licensed professionals. We can also offer low-toxicity and natural treatment options for sensitive households. Our technicians will provide specific safety instructions for before and after treatment.
